arts.ttl: Trafford saves TWO late Isidor pens as Burnley hold Sunderland
arts.sum: James Trafford staggeringly saved two late Wilson Isidor penalties as Burnley held Sunderland to a goalless draw.

arts.ttl: Burnley keeper Trafford saves two late penalties in stalemate with Sunderland
arts.sum: Burnley missed out on the chance to go top of the Championship but have goalkeeper James Trafford to thank for keeping them in second after he saved two late penalties from Wilson Isidor against Sunderland in a goalless draw. If Isidor had scored from the spot, it would have sent the Black Cats above the Clarets and a point behind the leaders Leeds. Burnley’s promotion push is starting to feel like a grind, having scored once in their past four Championship matches as they aim to make life hard for themselves in search of a return to the Premier League, although their defensive record is now a very boastful nine conceded in 27 games. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Burnley 0-0 Sunderland: Long unbeaten run continues thanks to James Trafford's late penalty heroics
arts.sum: James Trafford’s late penalty heroics ensured Burnley’s long-unbeaten run continued following a dramatic promotion showdown with Sunderland.

arts.ttl: James Trafford saves two late Sunderland penalties to earn Burnley a draw
arts.sum: Wilson Isidor wasted two chances to win it from the spot.

arts.ttl: Sunderland looking to sign Westerlo’s record signing despite inconsistent time in Belgium

arts.ttl: Burnley team news for crunch Sunderland clash as Mike Tresor misses out despite Reading return
arts.sum: Mike Tresor has been left out of Burnley’s squad for their crunch promotion showdown with Sunderland despite making his long-awaited return to action.

arts.ttl: FA Cup: Chelsea, Man Utd, Liverpool and Tottenham new dates revealed as fourth round TV schedule is announced

arts.ttl: Sunderland negotiations ‘ongoing’ – Player interested in €15m move, ‘big wish’ of Le Bris
arts.sum: On Thursday, we covered claims from Belgium which stated Matija Frigan would be open to joining Sunderland, and KVC Westerlo are willing to let him leave in the winter market. Germanijak state the Black Cats have stepped forward to sign the 21-year-old as they have ‘fallen in love’ with him.
